title: Computer Essentials
description: Fundamental computer skills, hardware, and system management

questions.question: Which of the following is a central processing unit (CPU)?
options: The main processor that executes instructions
questions.explanation: The CPU is the primary processor responsible for executing all instructions and operations in a computer.

questions.question: RAM (Random Access Memory) is permanent storage that keeps data even after shutdown.
questions.explanation: RAM is temporary memory that is cleared when the computer is turned off. Permanent storage uses hard drives or SSDs.

questions.question: What is the correct way to organize files on a computer?
options: Use folders and subfolders based on content type
Keep all files in the Downloads folder
questions.explanation: Organizing files in logical folder structures makes them easier to find, backup, and manage.

questions.question: What should you do regularly to maintain computer performance?
options: Update software and remove unnecessary files
questions.explanation: Regular updates and maintenance ensure security, performance, and compatibility of your system.

questions.question: Using the same password across multiple accounts is a secure practice.
questions.explanation: Using unique, strong passwords for each account significantly improves security and protects your data.

questions.question: Which of these is an external input device?
questions.explanation: A mouse is an input device used to control the cursor and interact with the computer.

questions.question: What does defragmentation do to a hard drive?
options: Organizes data more efficiently for faster access
Encrypts all data on the drive
questions.explanation: Defragmentation rearranges data on the disk to improve read/write speed and system performance.

questions.question: Hibernate mode uses more power than sleep mode.
questions.explanation: Hibernate uses minimal power as it saves the session to disk, while sleep maintains power to RAM.

questions.question: Which type of storage is fastest for accessing data?
questions.explanation: SSDs have no moving parts and provide significantly faster data access than traditional hard drives.

questions.question: Creating regular backups is optional and not necessary for data protection.
questions.explanation: Regular backups are essential to protect against data loss from hardware failure, malware, or accidents.
